ratings.comment: Professor that understands how the whole field works and what actually matters. Acknowledges the history and makes the content interesting. Very approachable as well!
A solid lecturer! His exams were much simpler than those in the second half of the class, and he explains things clearly.

Professor Payseur was a great professor. He really cared about students understanding genetics. Clear expectations about what you need to know, helpful homework, and willing to meet outside of class if you had any questions.

Good lecturer but tends to lean towards academic language in his explanations which can make it difficult to get to the base of what he's saying, at least when discussing probability. Seems to really care for the students, low-stress exams, and is always happy to answer questions.

Had professor Payseur for the first half of genetics 467 and I enjoyed his lectures! (put them on x2 speed because he talked so slow lol). He was organized and the tests were pretty fair. The class itself was a little more difficult than I thought it would be, and since it is not curved I had to work pretty hard to get my desired grade.
Taught in a way to peak our curiosity and encouraged discussion. Tests were low stress.
Super nice guy and definitely cares about genetics, but his questions are very specific on the exams. Make sure you know the trees and the forest!
